# Resource (hush_apigee_access_credential)

Manage Apigee dynamic access credentials in the Hush Security platform.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Create an Apigee access credential with a service account key
resource "hush_apigee_access_credential" "example" {
  name           = "my-apigee-credential"
  description    = "Apigee credential with service account key"
  deployment_ids = ["dep-1234567890"]

  service_account_key_wo         = file("service-account.json")
  service_account_key_wo_version = 1
}

# Create an Apigee access credential with provider credentials (no explicit key)
resource "hush_apigee_access_credential" "provider_creds" {
  name           = "my-apigee-provider-creds"
  description    = "Apigee credential using provider credentials"
  deployment_ids = ["dep-1234567890"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the Apigee access credential
- `deployment_ids` (List of String) List of deployment IDs that can access this credential

### Optional

- `description` (String) The description of the Apigee access credential
- `service_account_key` (String, Sensitive) The GCP service account key JSON content
- `service_account_key_wo` (String, Sensitive, Write-only) The GCP service account key JSON content (write-only). This is a write-only attribute that is more secure than `service_account_key` because Terraform will not store this value in the state file.
- `service_account_key_wo_version` (String) Used to trigger updates for `service_account_key_wo`. Required when using `service_account_key_wo`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the Apigee access credential
- `has_provider_credentials` (Boolean) Whether the credential uses provider credentials (no explicit service account key)
- `type` (String) The type of access credential
- `kind` (String) The kind of access credential
